content.text:
Here is what changed.

### 1. `HeaderUserInfo` / `AppHeader` (`packages/ui/src/components/app-header.tsx`)

`HeaderUserInfo` now supports optional:

- **`profileHref` / `profileLabel`** — Renders a **Mijn profiel** row with `UserIcon`. Uses `NavLink` when the header gets `NavLink`, otherwise a plain `<a>`.
- **`company`** — `{ name, description? }` — Renders an **Organisatie** block under the user lines (name + optional second line).

Both **desktop** (`DropdownMenu`) and **mobile** (`Sheet`) menus include the org block and profile entry. Extra separators separate profile from language where needed.

### 2. `AuthenticatedAppShell`

For a real session, the header user payload now includes:

- **`profileHref`** — From `PROTOTYPE_PRIMARY_NAV` (`user-profile` → `/app/user-profile`), with **`profileLabel`**: `"Mijn profiel"`.
- **`company`** — **`name`**: `user.homeOrganization.name` (company you’re logged in as). **`description`**: `Vertegenwoordigt: …` only when `representedOrganization.id !== homeOrganization.id`.

Also corrected the first breadcrumb link from **`/dashboard`** to **`/requests`** so “Workspace” matches the real default route.

The onboarding app **`bun run build`** succeeds with these updates. (`packages/ui`’s own `tsc` still reports unrelated story/sewdn issues that were already there.)

content.text:
Here is what was added and how it behaves.

### `HeaderUserInfo.company` (`packages/ui/src/components/app-header.tsx`)

- **`organizationProfileHref` / `organizationProfileLabel`** — Renders an **Organisatieprofiel** row with `CubeIcon` (same idea as the sidebar), using `NavLink` when the header provides `NavLink`.
- **`organizationSwitcher`** — Optional `{ organizations, activeOrganizationId, onSelectOrganization }`. If `organizations.length > 1`, the menu shows **Wissel van organisatie**:
  - **Desktop:** submenu with `DropdownMenuRadioGroup` + `Building02Icon` on the trigger.
  - **Mobile:** labeled list of buttons; active row shows `Tick01Icon`.

Separators before the language block run when any of user profile, org profile, or multi-org switcher is present.

### Mock session & auth (`ui-pt1-prototype`)

- **`MockPrototypeUser.organizations`** — Optional list of orgs the account can use (deduped by `id`). If omitted, behaviour is unchanged: only `homeOrganization`.
- **`MockPrototypeSession`** — Now includes **`activeOrganization`** and **`organizations`** (resolved list).
- **`mockPrototypeMembershipsForUser()`** — Exported helper to build that list (dedupe, default to home).
- **`MockPrototypeAuthProvider`** — Persists `{ userId, activeOrganizationId? }` in the same sessionStorage key; resolves `activeOrganization`; validates stored org id when the user or list changes.
- **`setActiveOrganization`** on context + **`useMockPrototypeSetActiveOrganization()`** hook (also available via **`useMockPrototypeAuth()`**).

### Onboarding app

- **`mock-prototype-users.ts`** — **Jane** has `organizations: [PROCERTUS, Acme]` so you can try switching.
- **`AuthenticatedAppShell`** — Sidebar workspace uses **`session.activeOrganization`**. Header company name / “Vertegenwoordigt” line uses **active** vs **represented** org ids. Passes profile URL, org profile URL from `PROTOTYPE_PRIMARY_NAV` (`organization-profile`), and the switcher when `session.organizations.length > 1`.
- **`reviewRequesterFromSession`** — “Workspace:” line uses **`session.activeOrganization.name`** when present.

Signing in as **Jane Doe** gives **Mijn profiel**, **Organisatieprofiel**, and **Wissel van organisatie** between PROCERTUS and Acme; the sidebar workspace name updates with the selection. Other demo users stay single-org (no switcher).

content.text:
Here is what was added.

### Route
- `/dashboard` now renders **`DashboardPage`** instead of the placeholder (`App.tsx`).

### Files
1. **`src/pages/dashboard-mock-data.ts`** — Static mock strings and numbers (user, org, requests, certificates, user management, orders, invoices, demo snippets).
2. **`src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`** — Full layout:
   - **Welcome hero** (full width): gradient background, Dutch welcome copy, and a **large** `/Procertus Logo with tagline.svg` in a frosted panel (`h-32` → `h-48` on large screens).
   - **12-column bento grid** with section labels aligned to the sidebar:
     - **Hoofdmenu**: Dashboard KPIs (4 tiles), **Mijn profiel** (mock Jane Doe), **Gebruikersbeheer** (12 users / 4 roles + role chips), **Organisatieprofiel** (mock PROCERTUS + VAT + **recente certificatie-aanvragen** list with status badges).
     - **Certificatie**: **Aanvragen** (badge summary), **Certificaten & Attesten** (counts + last document line).
     - **Financieel**: **Bestellingen**, **Facturen**.
     - **Demo**: **Organisatie**, **Beslissingsboom**, **Design system**.
     - **Onderaan in de zijbalk**: compact links for **Help** and **Settings** from `PROTOTYPE_SECONDARY_NAV` (same URLs as the sidebar, `#` for now).

Each main bento tile uses the **same icon and title** as `navConfig` and an **Openen →** link to the matching route via `navItemByKey`.

### Design notes
- Cards share a light **hover** (border + shadow), icon in a **primary-tinted** rounded square, and responsive **column spans** (`col-span-12` with `lg:col-span-*`) for a bento-style rhythm.
- All metrics and lists are **fixed mocks**; nothing reads session or API data.

If you want the hero to use the vertical logo asset or a different tagline file, say which file under `public/` you prefer.

### Main region height & scrolling

- The layout root is `flex … overflow-hidden`; the **main slot** (`.detail-panels-main-view`) is a **`flex h-full min-h-0 flex-col overflow-hidden`** column so children receive a bounded height.
- **Do not** force `100svh` on content inside the main slot unless the app root is only this layout. Prefer **`h-full min-h-0`** so the shell fills the measured panel width/height.

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
1. **`ManagementAppShell`** used **`h-svh`** on `SidebarProvider`, so the shell always tried to be **viewport** height even when it lives inside **`PanelsLayout`’s main column** (which has a **smaller** width/height budget next to docked panels). That breaks the usual `min-h-0` → **bounded flex child** chain.
2. **`<main>`** used **`overflow-hidden`**, so tall routes never scrolled inside the shell; with `html/body/#root { overflow: hidden }` in globals, **nothing** could scroll.
3. The header “scrolled” shadow listened to **`window.scrollY`**, but scroll is **not** on the window in this setup, so that signal was wrong (secondary issue).

### Fixes

**`packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/management-app-shell.tsx`**
- `SidebarProvider`: **`h-svh` → `h-full min-h-0 w-full max-h-full overflow-hidden`** so the shell **fills the panels main slot** and participates in flex shrink correctly.
- **`<main>`**: **`overflow-hidden` → `overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden`** so **route content scrolls inside `<main>`** (with `min-h-0 flex-1` unchanged).
- Scroll listener: **`ref` on `<main>`** and **`scrollTop`** instead of `window.scrollY`.

**`packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/Panels/Panels.tsx`**
- **Main slot** (`.detail-panels-main-view`): **`h-full overflow-auto` → `flex h-full min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-col overflow-hidden`** so children get a **fixed-height column** and **one** vertical scroll surface (the shell’s `<main>`), not a second competing scroll on the panels wrapper.

**`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/layouts/AuthenticatedAppShell.tsx`**
- Wrapped **`AppPanelsLayout`** in **`<div className="h-full min-h-0 w-full">`** so height **`100%`** is defined under `AlertDialogProvider` (which does not add a layout node).

**`PANELS_AI_GUIDE.md`**
- Added a short **“PanelsLayout (sewdn)”** section: main slot is a flex column with **`min-h-0`**, avoid **`100svh`** inside it, and put **`overflow-y-auto`** on the app shell’s primary scroll surface.

Onboarding **`bun run build`** passes with these updates.

content.text:
Here is what changed in `DashboardPage.tsx`, aligned with the spacing guidelines (micro → component → section → region) and the patterns in `spacing-guidelines.mdx` / `spacing-radix.stories.tsx`:

### Page frame
- Root: `flex flex-col gap-region` instead of `space-y-8` — **region** rhythm between the welcome block and the grid (per “between top-level page blocks”).
- Grid: `gap-section` instead of `gap-4 md:gap-5` — **section** for gaps between cards/siblings in the layout (same idea as the stat row in the guidelines).

### Welcome header
- `pb-region` under the divider; inner layout `gap-region` on large screens.
- Title block: `flex flex-col gap-micro` so eyebrow, title, and body use **micro** “inside a phrase” spacing.

### Flat cards (`Card` / `CardHeader` / `CardContent`)
- Headers use **`gap-component`** (icon/avatar rows, org block, certification header) and **`pb-0`** so vertical rhythm comes from the card’s built-in **`gap-component`** between header and content, not extra arbitrary padding.
- Title + subtitle stacks: **`gap-micro`** or **`space-y-micro`**.
- **`DashboardNavCard`**: `gap-component` in the header row, **`space-y-micro`** for title/description, **`gap-section`** in `CardContent`.
- Certification main content: **`gap-section`** on the two-column grid; left column uses **`gap-section`** between blocks and **`gap-component`** inside each block; status list **`gap-micro`**; preview list **`gap-component`**; row links **`px-component py-component`**; badge row **`mt-micro`** + **`gap-component`**.
- Certificate teaser: **`p-section`** + **`gap-section`** (inside a panel).
- Finance card: header **`gap-micro`**; body **`gap-section`** between rows; row label/value **`gap-component`**; footer links **`gap-section`**.

### Secondary nav
- Container **`gap-section`**; each link **`gap-micro`** (icon ↔ label), **`px-section py-component`** (section padding horizontally, component vertically — matches “component padding” with a bit more horizontal air on pills).

Semantic classes resolve through `--spacing-*` in `default.css`, so they follow operational vs spacious density without extra logic. Build passes.

content.text:
Here is what changed:

### More air on each flat card
- **`flatCardClass`** now includes **`p-section`** and a light **`bg-muted/10`** (with **`dark:bg-muted/5`**) so each tile has a clear **section**-scale inset on all sides. That overrides the default **`py-component`** on `Card` via `cn`, so vertical padding matches the horizontal token instead of feeling tight.

### No double horizontal padding
- **`flatCardChromeClass`** is **`px-0`** on every flat **`CardHeader`** and **`CardContent`**, so you are not stacking the shell’s **`p-section`** with the primitives’ default **`px-component`**.

### More space between tiles
- The bento grid uses **`gap-region`** instead of **`gap-section`** so cards sit a bit farther apart.

### Accidental truncation fixed
- The **Demo** nav cards, **Design system** card, and **Onderaan in de zijbalk** + **`PROTOTYPE_SECONDARY_NAV`** block were missing after a bad edit; that JSX is restored so the page matches the intended layout again.

Build and lints are clean. If you want the tiles even looser, the next step would be **`p-section md:p-region`** on `flatCardClass` only at `md+` (still token-based).

Here is what was implemented.

### `dashboard-mock-data.ts`

Here’s what was added and how the layout works.

### `dashboard-mock-data.ts`
- **`DashboardNotificationModule`**: `"user-management" | "certification" | "invoicing"`.
- **`DashboardNotification`**: `id`, `module`, `message`, `occurredAt` (ISO).
- **`mockRecentNotifications()`**: five sample rows (user activation, cert approved/submitted, new invoice, user added), sorted newest first.

### `RecentNotificationsWidget.tsx`
- Same **card header** pattern as certificatie/facturen: **`Notification01Icon`** in the **`size-10` / `rounded-xl` / `bg-primary/8`** tile, title **“Meldingen”**, short description.
- **`h4`**: **“Recente meldingen”** (same utility stack as other section labels).
- Each row: **`Item`** `variant="muted"` `size="sm"` with **`ItemMedia`** (module icon from **`navItemByKey`**: gebruikersbeheer, aanvragen, facturen), **`ItemTitle`** = message, **`ItemDescription`** = **`nl-BE`** date + time (`day`, `short` month, `hour`, `minute`).
- **`ItemTitle`** uses **`line-clamp-none`** so longer lines wrap instead of single-line clamp.
- List area: **`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto`** so extra items scroll inside the tile on tall bento rows.

### `DashboardPage.tsx` (bento height)
- Grid: **`items-stretch`** so all columns share the row height.
- **Left column** (`lg:col-span-4`): **`flex flex-col gap-region lg:h-full lg:min-h-0`**.
  - **`SessionContextWidget`**: **`shrink-0`** (natural height).
  - **`RecentNotificationsWidget`**: **`min-h-0 flex-1`** (fills the rest of the column so **profile + meldingen** together match the height of the cert and invoice columns).
- **Certificatie** wrapper: **`lg:h-full flex min-h-0`** + widget **`className="min-h-0 w-full flex-1"`**.
- **Facturen**: **`lg:h-full min-h-0`** on the widget (still **`h-full`** on the card).

### `dashboard-widgets/index.ts`
- Re-exports **`RecentNotificationsWidget`** and its props type.

If you want the meldingen tile when there is no session/user, we can render it outside the `user && contextOrganization` guard next.

<user_query>
Why are the bento grid widgets currently so large in height?
</user_query>

Checking the dashboard grid and widget height classes to explain what's driving the tall layout.

[REDACTED]

Here’s **why** those tiles end up so tall:

### 1. **The row is as tall as the tallest column**
The bento row is a **12-column grid**. Row height is driven by the **tallest** of the three columns (usually **Certificatie**: header, list, “Actieve certificaten” strip, two links). The grid does **not** shrink each column to its own content; it uses one shared row height.

### 2. **`items-stretch` (and default grid stretch)**
With **`items-stretch`**, every column **stretches vertically** to that shared row height. So the left column and the invoice column get the **same height** as the certification column, even if their own content is shorter.

### 3. **We then fill that height on purpose**
Earlier we aligned the bento by:

- **`lg:h-full`** on the left stack, certification wrapper, and invoice widget so they **fill the grid cell**.
- **`h-full`** on the **Certificatie** and **Facturen** cards (they grow to the full cell).
- **`flex-1 min-h-0`** on **Recent notifications** so it **takes all space under the profile** in the left column.

So the tiles are “large” because **(a)** the row is at least as tall as the certification block, and **(b)** we **stretch** the other cards (and flex the notifications card) to **use that full height** instead of staying content-sized.

---

**If you want shorter tiles:** use **`items-start`** (columns only as tall as their content, heights can differ), and/or drop **`h-full` / `flex-1`** so cards hug their content. That trades the strict “all columns same height” bento for a more compact layout.

### What we aligned with onboarding / wizard
- **`CustomerContext`** (`anonymous-onboarding-types.ts`) drives the anonymous flow: **representative** (voornaam, achternaam, aanhef preset/tekst, e-mail, functie preset/label), **organisation** (naam, land, BTW), and **structured address** (straat, huisnummer, postcode, plaats). The same object is summarized in **`onboardingReviewRequesterFromContext`** (`anonymous-onboarding-flow-helpers.tsx`) for **`RequestPackageReview`** on the review step.
- **Authenticated wizard** uses **`reviewRequesterFromSession`** for the same review block — built from **`MockPrototypeUser`** + active org (not the full `CustomerContext`).

### New utility
- **`src/features/profile/readStoredOnboardingContext.ts`** — reads **`pt1:onboarding-flow-state`** and, when present, **`context`** inside **`readOnboardingRegistrationCompletePayload().flowStateSnapshot`**. Exports **`customerContextsEqual`** so we don’t duplicate identical flow vs snapshot blocks.

### New pages (same shell as dashboard: `max-w-[1400px]`, padding, **`PageHeader`** + nav icon in media)
1. **`UserProfilePage.tsx`** (`/user-profile`)  
   - **Sessie:** `id`, `displayName`, `email`, `role`, thuis- / vertegenwoordigde organisatie.  
   - **Organisaties in sessie** (when `session.organizations` exists).  
   - **Vertegenwoordiger (onboardingflow):** all **`CustomerContext`** person fields from storage (flow + optional distinct registration snapshot).

2. **`OrganizationProfilePage.tsx`** (`/organization-profile`)  
   - **Actieve organisatie:** naam, id, **`mockOrganizationAddress`**, **`mockOrganizationUserCount`**.  
   - **Context uit account:** thuis vs vertegenwoordigd.  
   - **Beschikbare organisaties.**  
   - **Bedrijf (onboardingflow):** company-side **`CustomerContext`** fields (naam, BTW, land, adres).

3. **`pages/profile/DataRow.tsx`** — small **`dl`‑style** row helper.

### **`App.tsx`**
- Routes **`/user-profile`** and **`/organization-profile`** now render these pages (placeholders removed). Imports added for **`UserProfilePage`** and **`OrganizationProfilePage`**.

Here is what was implemented.

### Effect schemas (`packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/schema/prototype-profile-schemas.ts`)
- **`PrototypePostalAddressSchema`** / **`PrototypePostalAddress`**
- **`PrototypeOrganizationRefSchema`** / **`PrototypeOrganizationRef`** (session/workspace row: `id` + `name`)
- **`PrototypeOrganizationProfileSchema`** / **`PrototypeOrganizationProfile`** — full org: trade/legal name, VAT, enterprise number, contacts, website, registered + optional correspondence address, sector, headcount, founded year
- **`PrototypeUserProfileSchema`** / **`PrototypeUserProfile`** — person: names, emails, phones, job/department, locale/time zone/language, salutation, employee ref, role, notes
- **`MockPrototypeUserSchema`** / **`MockPrototypeUser`** — `PrototypeUserProfile` + `homeOrganization`, `representedOrganization`, optional `organizations`
- **`MockPrototypeSessionSchema`** / **`MockPrototypeSession`**
- **`formatPrototypePostalAddressLine`**

Shared field definitions use a single `prototypeUserProfileFields` object so **`MockPrototypeUserSchema`** stays aligned with **`PrototypeUserProfileSchema`**.

### Mock data
- **`src/data/mock-prototype-users.ts`** — `MOCK_PROTOTYPE_USERS` decoded with **`Schema.decodeUnknownSync(Schema.Array(MockPrototypeUserSchema))`** (invalid fixtures fail at load).
- **`src/data/mock-prototype-organization-profiles.ts`** — full profiles for `org-procertus`, `org-acme`, `org-greenleaf`, `org-northwind` + **`getPrototypeOrganizationProfile`**.

### Types & auth
- **`types/mock-prototype-user.ts`** — re-exports **`MockPrototypeUser`**, **`MockPrototypeSession`**, **`MockPrototypeOrganization`** (= ref); keeps **`mockPrototypeMembershipsForUser`**.
- **`types/mock-prototype-profile-patch.ts`** — **`MockPrototypeUserProfilePatch`** = **`Partial<Omit<PrototypeUserProfile, "id">>`** so patches follow the same entity.
- **`MockPrototypeAuthProvider`** — merges any patch keys onto the person slice (with org refs still driven by name overrides); **`users`** prop is **`readonly MockPrototypeUser[]`** so the decoded readonly array type-checks.

### Public API (`@procertus-ui/ui-pt1-prototype`)
Exports schema **types** and **values** (`*Schema`, `formatPrototypePostalAddressLine`, `MOCK_PROTOTYPE_USERS`, `MOCK_PROTOTYPE_ORGANIZATION_PROFILES`, `getPrototypeOrganizationProfile`, …).

### App usage
- **`main.tsx`** — imports **`MOCK_PROTOTYPE_USERS`** from the package; removed **`apps/.../data/mock-prototype-users.ts`**.
- **`dashboard-mock-data.ts`** — **`mockOrganizationAddress`** uses **`getPrototypeOrganizationProfile`** + **`formatPrototypePostalAddressLine`**; **`mockOrganizationUserCount`** prefers **`employeeCountApprox`** from the org profile when present.
- **`UserProfilePage`** — imports **`PrototypeUserProfile`** / **`MockPrototypeUser`**; title prefers **`givenName` + `familyName`**; session card lists all profile fields; edit form and save pass a full **`MockPrototypeUserProfilePatch`** (minus onboarding keys, still merged into localStorage as before).
- **`OrganizationProfilePage`** — imports **`PrototypeOrganizationProfile`** and renders **`OrganizationDatasetSummaryRows`** when a dataset profile exists for the active org id.

### Storybook
- **`mock-prototype-passwordless-login-form.stories.tsx`** and **`mock-prototype-user-select.stories.tsx`** use **`MOCK_PROTOTYPE_USERS`** from package data instead of partial inline fixtures.

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### Stepped demo modals (onboarding-style)
- **`OrganizationProfilePage`** and **`UserProfilePage`** now open a **`Dialog`** whose body uses **`StepLayout`** + horizontal **`OnboardingStepper`** from `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`, aligned with the anonymous onboarding pattern (`variant="wizard"`, `layout="fill-parent"`).
- Navigation: **Annuleren**, **Vorige**, **Volgende** / **Opslaan** on the last step. Form state is filled when you click **Gegevens bewerken** (no `useEffect` sync that triggered cascading-render lint).

### Organization edit (3 steps)
1. **Werkruimte** — sessienaam, demo-adres, read-only org-id.  
2. **Bedrijf** — `CustomerContext` fields (naam, BTW, land, adres) → `mergeStoredOnboardingCustomerContext` + naam in sessie.  
3. **Aanvullend** — optional **referentiedataset** fields: handels/statutaire naam, ondernemingsnr., e-mail, telefoon, website, sector, medewerkers, oprichtingsjaar, **geregistreerd** en **correspondentie** adres (ISO landcode). Address blocks are only sent in the patch when at least one field is non-empty, so you do not wipe the mock dataset with blanks.

### User edit (4 steps)
1. **Identiteit** — weergavenaam, voor-/achternaam, e-mail, rol (with short hint for optional role).  
2. **Contact & werk** — werk-e-mail, telefoon, mobiel, functie, afdeling, locale, tijdzone, taal, aanhef, personeelsref., notities.  
3. **Organisaties** — thuis- en vertegenwoordigde org **weergavenamen**.  
4. **Vertegenwoordiger** — onboarding `CustomerContext` person fields.

### Prototype persistence for org “dataset” overlays
- **`MockPrototypeProfilePatch`** now supports `organizationProfiles?: Record<string, MockPrototypeOrganizationProfilePatch>`.
- **`MockPrototypeAuthProvider`** persists **`organizationProfilePatches`** in the same sessionStorage key as user/org name overrides, and exposes **`resolveOrganizationProfile(organizationId)`** on context (merged static mock + patch).
- **`mergePrototypeOrganizationProfile`** + **`MockPrototypeOrganizationProfilePatch`** live in `packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/lib/merge-prototype-organization-profile.ts` (undefined keys do not clobber the base).
- **`useMockPrototypeResolveOrganizationProfile()`** wraps the new resolver; the org profile card uses **`resolveOrganizationProfile(active.id)`** so edits show immediately after save.

### Exports
- From `@procertus-ui/ui-pt1-prototype`: `mergePrototypeOrganizationProfile`, `MockPrototypeOrganizationProfilePatch`, `useMockPrototypeResolveOrganizationProfile`.

`bun run --filter frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding build` succeeds. Post-registration “complete your profile” flow remains out of scope as requested; this is **edit-profile** only with optional fields surfaced in the modal.

When changing information for a user profile or an organization profile, it cannot be changed directly because it might impact other processes in Prosdeptis. Instead of directly updating the information, a change document is prepared and submitted as a change request.

We need to keep track of and persist these change requests for both types of profiles, and visualize which information has been altered and not yet approved. Like a certification request, the change request goes through a comparable lifecycle in which the change can be canceled.

A change request can be canceled, accepted, validated, or rejected. Notes can be added to these change requests from both ends of the conversation.

Here is what was completed to close out the profile change-request work.

### User profile page
- Replaced direct **`handleSave`** (patch + merge) with **`submitChangeRequest`**: builds a baseline via `buildUserProfileChangeBaseline`, flattens the form, blocks empty diffs, then **`createRequest`** with `kind: "user"` and `subjectUserId: user.id`.

- **`navConfig.ts`**: new item **“Profielwijzigingen”** under the **Certificatie** group (next to **Aanvragen**).

### Reliability fixes
- **`use-profile-change-requests.ts`**: `requests` is now **`useMemo(..., [version])`** so the list updates after every persist (previously the store was read once per render and stayed stale).
- **`flatten.ts`**: replaced **`for...of` on a `Set`** with a key union that avoids **`downlevelIteration`** / target issues during build.

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### One open request per subject
- New helpers in `profile-change-request-queries.ts`: `findPendingProfileChangeRequestForUser` / `findPendingProfileChangeRequestForOrganization` (pending = `submitted` or `validated`).
- `createRequest` refuses a second request for the same user or organization and returns `{ ok: false, reason: "…" }` (shown with `window.alert` from the profile pages).

### After merge or “done” → gone from the client
- **`acceptAndApply`**: applies the change, then **removes** the request from `localStorage` (no `accepted` row kept).
- **`setStatus(..., "rejected")` / `"canceled"`**: **removes** the request instead of keeping a terminal row.

So the UI only ever shows **open** work; approved/rejected/cancelled requests disappear from storage and from lists.

### Profile pages: one indicator + one block
- **Header**: badge **`Wijziging: {status}`** (Dutch labels from `PROFILE_CHANGE_STATUS_LABEL`) when there is a pending request.
- **`Gegevens bewerken`**: **disabled** while a pending request exists (tooltip explains why).
- **Body**: single short explanation + **one** `ProfileChangeRequestCard` (diff, notes, actions)—no extra warning card, no list.

### Profielwijzigingen overview
- List is **filtered to pending** only, so it stays aligned with “nothing to show after resolution.”
- Empty-state copy mentions submit paths and that finished requests drop off.

Build passes (`bun run --filter frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding build`).

**Note:** Older `localStorage` entries that were already `"accepted"` before this change are not auto-pruned; they are simply **hidden** on the overview (pending filter). If you want a one-time migration to strip non-pending rows from `pt1-profile-change-requests`, that can be added next.

content.text:
Here is what was added and how it behaves.

### Overview card (certification-style digest)
New `ProfileChangeRequestOverviewCard` mirrors the **certification request overview** pattern: compact `Card`, status **badge**, horizontal **`CertificationRequestLifecycleTimeline`** with dates from `profileChangeTimelineDateLabels`, and an **`ItemGroup`** digest of up to **three** changed fields (label + was → proposed). Footer line tells users to click for full detail.

### Detail panel
New `ProfileChangeDetailPanel` is registered as `profileChangeDetail` in `panelRegistry.ts` (alongside `requestDetail`). It uses **`CoverView`** + **`PanelSection`** like `RequestDetailPanel`, and includes:

- **Levenscyclus (overzicht)** — same horizontal timeline as the card, with dates  
- **Tijdlijn** — vertical **`CertificationRequestLifecycleDetailTimeline`** built from `profileChangeDetailLifecycleEvents`  
- **Volledige wijzigingen** — full diff table  
- **Conversatie** — note thread + dual **Textarea** add-note (aanvrager / verwerker) when still pending  
- **Acties** — validate / accept & merge / reject / cancel (panel **closes** after accept, reject, or cancel so it does not sit on a removed request)

Helpers live in `profile-change-timeline-labels.ts` and `profile-change-lifecycle-events.ts`.

### Profile pages & overview
- **`UserProfilePage`** / **`OrganizationProfilePage`**: header badge removed; **`Gegevens bewerken`** stays disabled while a pending change exists. One **`max-w-xl`** digest card; click runs `openPanel(PROFILE_CHANGE_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE, { requestId })`.  
- **`useEffect` + `useRef`**: when the pending request **disappears** (resolved from the panel), **`storedTick`** bumps so onboarding/org display refreshes after merge.  
- **`ProfileChangeRequestsPage`**: same overview cards + panel; duplicate accept/validate logic removed from the page.

Exports: `PROFILE_CHANGE_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE` and `ProfileChangeDetailPanel` from `panels/index.ts` (re-exported with the rest of `panels`).

Here is what changed.

### Merged preview (`flatten.ts`)
- Added **`MergedFieldRow`** and **`mergedProfileFieldRows(baseline, proposed)`**, which walks the **union of all keys**, sets **`merged`** to the proposed value when that key exists on `proposed` (same rule as apply), otherwise the baseline value, and sets **`changed`** when `baseline !== merged`.

### Detail panel table (`profile_change_detail_panel.tsx`)
- Section title **“Velden na doorvoer”** with a short explanation of the merged view.
- **Legend**: pill-style **“Gewijzigd”** plus a count line (*X van Y veld(en) wijzigt/wijzigen*).
- **Toggle**: *Alleen gewijzigde velden* / *Toon alle velden* so you can focus on diffs or scan the full merged snapshot.
- **Table** (wider min width for readability):
  - **Dot column**: primary dot for changed rows, muted dot for unchanged.
  - **Veld**: label + small **“gewijzigd”** badge when applicable.
  - **Goedgekeurd (huidig)** vs **Na doorvoer (samengevoegd)** with a **→** column between them for a clear old / new reading order.
- **Changed rows**: left **primary border**, light **primary background**, slightly stronger background on the “huidig” cell; merged cell uses **medium weight** when changed. Unchanged rows stay visually quiet.

Build and lints are clean.

content.text:
Here is what was added and how it fits together.

### `PortalChatWindow` (`@procertus-ui/ui-lib`)
New presentational block under `packages/ui-lib/src/components/portal-chat/`:

- **`portal-chat-types.ts`** — `PortalChatMessage` (`placement`: `requester` | `portal`), optional `composer`, `emptyContent`, etc.
- **`PortalChatWindow.tsx`** — Scrollable transcript (`ScrollArea`), bubbles (same layout as your old inline chat), optional `Separator` + **`Input`** composer (read-only by default). Built from `@procertus-ui/ui` primitives.

The **`shadcn-svelte-extras`** tree (`src/lib/components/ui/chat`) is not in this workspace (that reference folder has no `chat` files here), so this follows the same **pattern** as typical shadcn-style chats: **scrollable message list + footer composer**, aligned with your existing profile UI.

### Storybook
`PortalChatWindow.stories.tsx` — **Default**, **TallScroll**, **Empty**, **EditableComposer** (`title: "ui-lib/PortalChatWindow"`).

### Exports
`packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts` re-exports `PortalChatWindow` and its types.

### Certification requests
- **`request-mock-portal-thread.ts`** — Three NL lines (aanvrager + PROCERTUS), same shape as the profile mock (`side` → mapped to `placement`).

### Panels
- **`request_detail_panel.tsx`** — After **Levenscyclus**, new **Conversatie** `PanelSection` using `PortalChatWindow` + mock thread. **Regels en documentatie** copy no longer mentions prototypes/mock downloads.
- **`profile_change_detail_panel.tsx`** — Inline `PortalChatPreview` removed; uses **`PortalChatWindow`** + existing `mockProfileChangePortalThread()` via a small mapper.

`bun run --filter @procertus-ui/ui-lib build` and `bun run --filter frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding build` both succeed.

Here is what was implemented.

### New conversation stack panel
- **`conversation-panel-config.ts`** — `CONVERSATION_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE` (`"conversationDetail"`) used as the registry key and in `openPanel`.
- **`conversation_detail_panel.tsx`** — Reads **`suite`** and **`scenario`** from panel props (synced to the URL via existing `useUrlQueryPersistence` / `panelProps`). **`Tabs`** switch scenarios; each change calls `openPanel(CONVERSATION_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE, { suite, scenario })` so the active scenario stays in the query string.
- **`features/conversations/conversation-detail-panel-mocks.ts`** — Suites `profileChange` / `certification`, scenarios `default` | `followUp` | `short`, plus **`CONVERSATION_SUITE_PROFILE_CHANGE`** / **`CONVERSATION_SUITE_CERTIFICATION`** for callers.

### Hook split (avoid circular imports)
- **`useAppPanels.ts`** — `usePanelsContext<AppPanelRegistry>()`; **`panelRegistry.ts`** only exports the registry + `AppPanelRegistry` type (no hook).
- **`panels/index.ts`** — Exports `useAppPanels`, `CONVERSATION_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE`, suite constants, registry, layouts, and all panels.

Here is what was finished and how it maps to your Svelte example.

### Shadcn-style toolbar (vs Svelte)
Your Svelte row uses **`Popover` + emoji UI + `Input` + submit `Button`**. In this repo that is implemented as **`ChatComposerToolbar`**: Radix/Shadcn **`Popover`**, **`Button`**, **`Input`**, plus a **search + preset emoji grid** (not a full `EmojiPicker` package—that would be an extra dependency). **`PortalChatWindow`** switches to it when `composer.toolbar` is truthy and the composer is not read-only.

### Changes just applied
- **`PortalChatWindow`**: `Separator` shows when there are messages **or** the toolbar is used, so the composer area is visually separated even with an empty list + toolbar.
- **`chat-composer-toolbar`**: Emoji buttons use stable keys (`emoji` + index) to avoid duplicate-key issues.
- **`portal-chat/index.ts`**: Restored **`export { ChatBubble }`** (it had been dropped), and kept exports for **`ChatComposerToolbar`**, **`ChatThreadHeader`**, **`CHAT_COMPOSER_DEFAULT_EMOJI_PRESETS`**, and **`PortalChatComposerToolbarOptions`** (with the rest of the portal-chat types).
- **`PortalChatFullExperience.stories.tsx`**: Two stories:
  - **`ComposedThread`** — full manual composition: **`ChatThreadHeader`** (avatar + call/video/info with Hugeicons), **`ChatList`**, bubbles + timestamps, typing bubble, **`ChatComposerToolbar`**.
  - **`PortalWindowWithToolbar`** — same flow using **`PortalChatWindow`** with `composer: { toolbar: true, ... }`.

4.  **Scrolling:** The `children` content is automatically wrapped in a `ScrollArea` by default (`scrollable={true}`).

## Fill-height / docked content (inner scroll)

When the main region should **stretch to the space below the cover header** and **only a nested component** (for example a chat message list) should scroll:

1. Set **`scrollable={false}`** on `CoverView` so the outer layout does not wrap children in `ScrollArea`.
2. In non-scrollable mode, the content slot is a **`flex flex-1 min-h-0 flex-col overflow-hidden`** region below the header spacer — design your `children` as a **column flex chain** (`flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col` on wrappers) down to the element that should scroll (`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto` or a component like `ChatList` that already uses that pattern).
3. Keep **`className="h-full"`** on `CoverView` when it sits inside a flex parent (e.g. a slide-over panel) so `flex-1` + `min-h-0` heights resolve correctly.
